summary: Justin Brennan demonstrates a 5-step system for sourcing off-market multifamily deals through direct broker outreach, cold calling, and relationship building. The video focuses heavily on deal sourcing methodology via broker calls in multiple markets (Ohio, Oklahoma, Texas), with secondary content on capital raising foundations, tax strategy (bonus depreciation), and entity structuring. However, it lacks specific underwriting frameworks or deal-analysis mechanics that would be directly actionable for Brian's stated drag points.
takeaways: Set up 'business in a box' foundation (LLC, website, CRM) before deal sourcing to build credibility with brokers and investors; without this, brokers won't trust you with deal flow
Build a 'list of 25-30' soft-committed capital partners in your CRM/database before getting deals under contract—this allows you to raise 3/4 of capital within 2 weeks when deal is locked
Use 'who do you know' framing instead of 'asking for money'; shift mentality from asking to providing opportunity (15-20% annual returns + tax write-offs via bonus depreciation)

Cold call brokers with buy-box definition (10-50 units, $2-3M price point for GA/TN/AL) to establish relationships; ask for referrals on lenders, property managers, insurance—brokers are 'gatekeepers' to local ecosystem
On auctions with reserve prices, ask broker directly: 'What's your whisper price/guidance?' (e.g., $50-60k/door comps); financeable auctions allow non-cash offers if you move quickly
Market trip: spend 3 days on ground touring neighborhoods, knowing rents/school zones/bad areas, meeting 2-3 brokers + 2-3 property managers + 2-3 lenders—do this before deal flow starts

Bonus depreciation: with 100% depreciation available in 2025, ~$1 cost seg study (post-close) yields almost dollar-for-dollar K1 write-off in year 1; spreads over 5 years; recapture minimal if held 5 years

skill_candidates.change: Adopt systematic cold-call script with buy-box definition rather than general inquiries; follow with referral asks for lenders/PMs/insurance to build local team before pursuing deal
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin's call to Zach in OKC specifies '10-50 units, $2-3M price point' and asks for lender/PM referrals; call to Beth Rose demonstrates asking broker for 'whisper price' comps and property manager vetting—both close with 'set meeting in-person January'

skill_candidates.change: Pre-populate CRM with 25-30 soft-committed capital partners and business foundation (website, LLC, financial projections) before pursuing deal flow, not after contract
skill_candidates.evidence: Julian example: locked first deal in 2 weeks and raised 3/4 of ~$500k in 2 weeks because 'all this foundation was set up'; Justin emphasizes 'If you're having to figure all this out while you're getting deals flowing in, the deals aren't going to happen'

skill_candidates.change: Obtain cost segregation pre-report for free from 3rd-party vendors (before or during escrow) to quantify bonus depreciation upside in underwriting model
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin: 'Cost seg companies can run a pre-report...they'll give you an idea of what...before you hire them, right, for free. They'll give you...a pre-report on what will likely be...you know, bonus depreciate about $2.7M, year 1 will look like this and years 2-5 will look like this'

title: Off-Market Apartment Deals Exposed | 5-Step System to Close Fast
